summary: Michael Roach Creative is a boutique, owner-led creative and digital marketing agency in Albany, NY offering content marketing, social media management, branding, video/audio production, WordPress consulting, and career/personal-branding coaching. It serves small-to-medium business owners and entrepreneurs who need professional marketing but lack the in-house time or skills, plus executives and career changers needing personal-brand clarity. Engagements are high-touch and custom-priced, sold via a free consultation that leads to a proposal and either a project or a monthly retainer.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new lead or freshly-signed client to first value fast — set expectations, build trust on Michael's credentials, and drive toward the first polished deliverable that makes them feel professionally represented.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation request submitted via the contact form, or a new client agreement signed. Send email 1 within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New contacts and new clients; branch on stated need (SMB ongoing marketing vs. personal-branding/career vs. one-off creative project).
campaigns.rationale: The brand has zero onboarding today and runs a fully manual, high-touch process, so an instant, warm welcome that confirms next steps prevents new leads from going cold between the form submission and Michael's personal reply — the single fastest engagement win.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, confirm what happens next, reduce the 'will a busy one-person shop respond?' anxiety, and quietly establish credibility — while giving one immediate value action.
emails.subject: Welcome to Michael Roach Creative — let's make you impossible to ignore
emails.preview: Here's exactly what happens next (and one thing you can do today).
emails.cta: Tell Michael about your business — reply or book your free consultation time
keyPoints: Thank them and set clear expectations: Michael personally reviews every request and will follow up to schedule the free consultation within one business day
Build quiet trust with credentials (PCM, Google Analytics Certified, AMA member; background with Citibank, NYSE, PBS) rather than hype
Give one quick win now: a short prompt to jot their #1 marketing goal so the consultation is productive
One human, low-friction CTA with a real reply-to address — reinforce this is a conversation, not a sales funnel

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Client Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a completed free consultation into a signed project or retainer by delivering the custom proposal, neutralizing the budget/pricing objection, and giving a clear, low-friction yes.
campaigns.trigger: Free consultation call completed; lead has not yet signed. Same-day follow-up, then behavior-based nudges if no response.
campaigns.segment: Post-consult leads; branch by service discussed (social/content retainer vs. branding/video project vs. personal-branding coaching).
campaigns.rationale: This is the core revenue play and the brand's biggest leak: pricing is opaque and SMBs are price-sensitive, so a same-day recap that frames cost against outcomes — and addresses 'will an outside agency get my business?' — is what actually closes the deal Michael already worked to earn on the call.

emails.purpose: Recap the consultation, deliver the custom proposal, and directly defuse the cost objection by tying price to the visibility/credibility outcome they came for — making the next step a single easy decision.
emails.subject: Your custom marketing plan — and what it'll actually cost
emails.preview: Recapping what we discussed, plus the proposal and clear next step.
emails.cta: Review your proposal & approve to get started
keyPoints: Recap their specific goal and the plan discussed on the call to prove Michael understood their business — directly answering the 'will an agency get my niche?' objection
Present pricing transparently and frame it against the outcome (consistent professional content, getting found, looking credible) rather than as a line-item cost
Offer flexibility — project vs. monthly retainer, start small and expand — to lower budget pressure for a price-sensitive SMB
Make saying yes effortless: one CTA to approve, plus an open invitation to reply with any question before deciding

campaigns.name: Consultation Booking & Reminders
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended free consultations and recover the ones that slip, so Michael's high-touch sales calls actually happen and aren't lost to no-shows.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation time confirmed; reminder cadence runs up to the call, with a no-show re-book branch.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled free consultation.
campaigns.rationale: The entire sales motion hinges on the free consultation actually happening, yet there's no confirmation or reminder system today — and reminders cut no-shows ~35%, directly protecting the top of the revenue funnel for a solo operator whose time is the bottleneck.

emails.purpose: Reduce no-shows with a 24-hour-before reminder that re-confirms the time, primes them to come prepared, and removes friction to reschedule rather than ghost.
emails.subject: Your free consultation with Michael is tomorrow at [time]
emails.preview: Quick confirmation, plus how to make the most of our 30 minutes.
emails.cta: Add to calendar — or reschedule in one click
keyPoints: Re-confirm date, time, and how the call happens (phone/video link) so nothing is ambiguous
Prime them to get value: suggest having their #1 marketing challenge and current website/socials in mind so Michael can give tailored advice on the call
Offer a frictionless reschedule link to recover would-be no-shows instead of losing them entirely
Reassure it's a no-pressure conversation about their needs, reinforcing the collaborative 'let's explore what you need' tone

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Turn lead-magnet downloaders into consultation requests by delivering the asset, teaching real value, and positioning Michael as the trusted expert over a multi-week cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Download of the Personal Branding audio course or the WordPress 1-hour video course.
campaigns.segment: Leads below the sales-ready threshold; branch by magnet topic (personal branding/career vs. WordPress/SMB marketing).
campaigns.rationale: Two lead magnets already exist but feed into nothing — capturing those downloaders into a nurture sequence converts otherwise-wasted top-of-funnel interest into booked consultations, and the educational format fits Michael's teach-don't-hype voice perfectly.

emails.purpose: Deliver the requested course immediately and frame the bigger problem it points to, so the download becomes the start of a relationship rather than a dead end.
emails.subject: Your Personal Branding course is inside — start with Lesson 1
emails.preview: Plus the mistake most people make before they ever fix their brand.
keyPoints: Deliver the asset instantly with a clear link and a one-line 'start here' to drive immediate consumption
Frame the real problem behind the topic — a strong personal brand (or site) only works when message, visuals, and consistency line up — setting up future emails
Establish authority lightly with Michael's credentials and the kinds of clients/outcomes he's delivered
Plant the soft next step: when they're ready to go beyond the course, a free consultation is one reply away

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind with warm leads, past clients, and Michael's referral network — building authority and assisting conversions without discounting.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding and route the long-dormant to a future win-back flow.
campaigns.rationale: This boutique runs on referrals and repeat/expansion work, so a steady, useful newsletter is the always-on engine that keeps Michael front-of-mind between projects — the cheapest insurance against the brand's top churn risk of being forgotten during slow seasons.

emails.purpose: Flagship issue establishing the recurring value habit — one genuinely useful, locally-relevant marketing idea SMB owners can act on, with a single soft CTA.
emails.subject: The one thing most Albany business owners get wrong on social media
emails.preview: A 5-minute fix that makes your posts actually get seen.
emails.cta: Read the full breakdown on the blog
keyPoints: Lead with one concrete, actionable tip relevant to Capital Region SMBs — 80% value, no pitch
Use a recurring named section (e.g., 'This Month's Visibility Tip') to build reader habit and brand recognition
Soft 20% offer: a one-line reminder that Michael can handle this for them, with a free-consultation link
Keep the voice educational and jargon-free, and invite replies to surface client questions for future issues
